name: fridge-magnets
description: Interactive fridge magnet board — drag and rearrange word cards, add your own words, and submit favorite arrangements to a Google Form.

activation.description: Place a ```fridge-magnets code fence in markdown. Cards pre-load from the 'cards' string. All boards support add-a-word and drag. Feedback boards also support submission.

cards.description: Pre-loaded card text. Use [text] for auto-layout or [text](x,y) for positioned cards. Custom (user-added) cards use [+text](x,y) in serialized output.

height.description: Initial canvas area height in pixels.

board.description: Human-readable board name. Automatically submitted as the 'board' metadata field when feedback-allow is yes.

show-editor.description: Show the YAML textarea editor on load. Default (false/no): editor is hidden, toggled via the ⌨ button.

feedback-allow.description: Enable submission buttons. Requires feedback-gform-url and feedback-fields.

feedback-gform-url.description: Google Form formResponse URL for submissions. Example: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/FORM_ID/formResponse

feedback-fields.description: Maps Google Form entry IDs to their semantic roles. All five keys required for full functionality. name and category may be omitted to hide those fields.

properties.board: entry.XXXXXXXXX — board name from the 'board' YAML field
properties.name: entry.XXXXXXXXX — submitter name (optional; omit to hide the name input)
properties.category: entry.XXXXXXXXX — category label chosen in modal (optional; omit to hide picker)

mode.description: Set to 'display-feedback' to render a read-only display of submitted arrangements from a Google Sheets CSV.

feedback-gsheet-csv.description: Google Sheets published CSV URL. Required when mode: display-feedback. Example: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/SHEET_ID/export?format=csv

feedback-moderation-hours.description: Hours after submission_at during which a row shows automatically (before manual approval). Rows with approved=yes always show; approved=no never show.
